articles.title: Putin visits China to reaffirm Russia ties
articles.summary: The Kremlin has said Putin and Xi plan to discuss economic cooperation between the two countries, but also "key international and regional issues."

articles.title: San Diego Mosque Shooting: What to Know About the Suspects and What Happened
articles.summary: Three men were killed in the attack at the Islamic Center of San Diego. Two teenage suspects were found dead in a car nearby, police said.

articles.title: Trump’s New $1.8 Billion Pot of Money, and a Deadly Mosque Attack in California
articles.summary: Plus, a North Korean soccer team hits the road.

articles.title: Some plants have a genetic superpower that may help them survive a cataclysm
articles.summary: Get ready for a biology lesson. Certain plants have extra sets of chromosomes. And it turns out, it's a useful trait for a species facing a dramatic event like climate change.

articles.title: The Race to Control Congress
articles.summary: We look at where the parties stand leading up to the midterms.

articles.title: The missing men of the American marriage market
articles.summary: A new study suggests the growing educational and economic divide between men and women is reshaping marriage and family life in America — leaving many women with a shrinking pool of economically stabl

articles.title: G.O.P. Supporters Back Trump, but a Third Seek a New Direction for the Party
articles.summary: While President Trump remains overwhelmingly popular within the Republican coalition, a New York Times/Siena poll found, a sizable share wants the party’s next nominee to take a different approach.

articles.title: The French Open courts are clay, a tricky surface for some. Here's how the pros do it
articles.summary: In tennis, clay has a reputation for being one of the harder surfaces to play on. But a few pros shared some of their tips for staying sharp.

articles.title: NTSB hearing will probe cause of fiery UPS jet crash that killed 15 in Louisville
articles.summary: The nation's top safety investigators will launch a two-day hearing beginning Tuesday into what caused a UPS cargo plane to crash shortly after takeoff in Louisville last year, killing 15 people.

articles.title: House holds off on prediction market ban despite bipartisan calls for prohibition
articles.summary: Minority Leader Jeffries is urging Speaker Johnson to "swiftly" hold vote on House prediction market ban.

articles.title: What we know about how the U.S. government uses spyware (and what we don't)
articles.summary: Critics of spyware, which can be used to remotely hack into phones, worry the Trump administration is eroding policies that stigmatized the commercial spyware industry.

articles.title: 'We're not kids anymore': The DACA generation hits their 30s with an unstable future
articles.summary: Recipients of the Obama-era DACA program are aging, even as the Trump administration moves to weaken the program's protections and benefits.

articles.title: These men voted for President Trump. They have very different views of how he's doing
articles.summary: Two Black men from Georgia who voted for President Trump in 2024 have very different views of how the country is doing now, in the first installment of Swing Shift from NPR's Tamara Keith.

articles.title: The LIRR Strike Is Over. Here’s What Commuters Can Expect on Tuesday.
articles.summary: A deal was reached on Monday night to end the strike that shut down America’s busiest passenger rail line, but officials said the service will not fully resume until Tuesday afternoon.

articles.title: One Palestinian man's life of survival
articles.summary: A 90-year-old Palestinian man in Gaza says his life has never felt safe as he recalls displacements and wars

articles.title: Trump says he's called off Iran strike at request of Gulf allies
articles.summary: President Donald Trump says he is holding off on a military strike on Iran planned for Tuesday because "serious negotiations" are underway to end the war.

articles.title: Last-Minute Donation Could Upend G.O.P. Primary for Texas Attorney General
articles.summary: A single donor’s contribution of $2.75 million could help propel Representative Chip Roy in his clash with a self-funded state senator, Mayes Middleton.

articles.title: A Fearful Phone Call and Frantic Search Preceded San Diego Mosque Shooting
articles.summary: San Diego police arrived to find three people dead. Nearby, they found two teenagers, the attackers, dead in a car. The violence shattered an idyllic Southern California city.

articles.title: Justices Hint at Strains as Supreme Court Comes Under Scrutiny
articles.summary: Traveling across the country, justices defend the role of the court even as strained relations among its members emerge in writing and remarks.

articles.title: Israeli Navy intercepts activists trying to break Gaza blockade with aid ships
articles.summary: On Monday, the Israeli navy intercepted a flotilla of 54 boats of activists headed to Gaza with humanitarian aid.

articles.title: Sen. Bill Cassidy loses primary. And, WHO declares Ebola outbreak a global emergency
articles.summary: Sen. Bill Cassidy, who voted to convict Trump, lost the Republican primary in Louisiana. And, the WHO has declared a global health emergency over a new Ebola outbreak.

articles.title: Pop star Shakira is acquitted in a Spanish tax fraud case
articles.summary: The decision follows years of tax troubles in Spain for the Colombian superstar. Spanish tax authorities did not prove that the singer was a resident of Spain, the court said in its decision.

articles.title: Ukraine launches large-scale drone strikes on Russia
articles.summary: As peace talks stall, Ukraine launches one of the biggest drone strikes against Russia since the beginning of Russia's full-scale invasion in an attempt to weaken Russia's war machine.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
